name: rate-limits
context: OpenAI API rate limits — RPM, TPM, and RPD by model and tier, with retry strategies and exponential backoff
info: OpenAI enforces rate limits per organization at the model level. Limits are expressed in requests per minute (RPM), tokens per minute (TPM), and requests per day (RPD). Limits vary by model (GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, o1, etc.) and usage tier (Free, Tier 1-5). Exceeding limits returns HTTP 429 with `x-ratelimit-*` headers showing remaining quota. The official SDKs (Node.js `openai`, Python `openai`) include automatic retry with exponential backoff for 429 and 5xx errors. For high-throughput applications, implement client-side rate limiting, request batching, and the Batch API for non-real-time workloads. Monitor usage at platform.openai.com/account/limits. Tier upgrades happen automatically as spend increases.
triggers: implement retry with backoff for openai
what are the openai rate limits

sections.title: Rate limit types
sections.body: RPM (requests per minute): total API calls per 60-second window. TPM (tokens per minute): combined input and output tokens per 60-second window. RPD (requests per day): total calls per 24-hour window. IPM (images per minute): for DALL-E endpoints. Both RPM and TPM must be satisfied — whichever limit is hit first triggers throttling. Limits are enforced per-organization, not per-API-key.

sections.title: Usage tiers
sections.body: Tiers range from Free to Tier 5 based on cumulative spend and account age. Upgrades are automatic. Free tier has very low limits (3 RPM). Tier 1 starts at $5 spent and provides 500 RPM. Each higher tier increases limits significantly. Check your current tier at platform.openai.com/account/limits. Tier requirements include both minimum spend and minimum account age.

sections.title: Rate limit headers
sections.body: Every API response includes `x-ratelimit-limit-requests`, `x-ratelimit-limit-tokens`, `x-ratelimit-remaining-requests`, `x-ratelimit-remaining-tokens`, `x-ratelimit-reset-requests`, and `x-ratelimit-reset-tokens`. Use these headers to implement proactive throttling before hitting 429 errors. The reset values indicate when the limits will refresh.

sections.title: Retry strategies
sections.body: The official SDKs retry automatically on 429 and 5xx errors (default 2 retries with exponential backoff). Configure with `maxRetries` option. For custom retry logic, implement exponential backoff with jitter: delay = 2^attempt * 1000 + random(0-1000)ms. Always use jitter to avoid thundering herd. For high-throughput: combine client-side rate limiting with server-side retry. The Batch API avoids rate limits entirely for non-real-time workloads.

sections.title: Best practices
sections.body: 1) Use `maxRetries` in SDK config instead of custom retry. 2) Implement client-side rate limiting below your actual limit (e.g., 90% of RPM). 3) Monitor `x-ratelimit-remaining-*` headers proactively. 4) Use GPT-4o-mini for high-volume tasks (higher TPM limits). 5) Use the Batch API for offline processing (50% cheaper, no rate limits). 6) Cache responses for repeated queries. 7) Use shorter prompts to stay within TPM limits.
